statement.focus_question: Let $f(1)=f(2)=1$ and for $n>2$\[f(n) = f(n-f(n-1))+f(n-f(n-2)).\]Does $f(n)$ miss infinitely many integers?
statement.normalized: Let $f(1)=f(2)=1$ and for $n>2$\[f(n) = f(n-f(n-1))+f(n-f(n-2)).\]Does $f(n)$ miss infinitely many integers? What is its behaviour?
statement.original: Let $f(1)=f(2)=1$ and for $n>2$\[f(n) = f(n-f(n-1))+f(n-f(n-2)).\]Does $f(n)$ miss infinitely many integers? What is its behaviour?
